title: Deutsche Telekom and Qunnect Successfully Test Quantum Teleportation Over Live Berlin Network
why:
- Quantum teleportation was successfully demonstrated over 30 kilometers of commercial fiber in Berlin, achieving a 90% average teleportation fidelity, marking a significant step towards real-world quantum networking.
- The field trial used commercially available quantum entanglement distribution hardware integrated into an operational metro fiber network, running alongside classical data traffic.
- This demonstration proves that quantum teleportation can be deployed by telecom operators on existing fiber infrastructure, paving the way for future quantum internet and secure quantum communication services.
- The teleportation was performed at a wavelength (795nm) critical for connecting neutral-atom quantum computers, atomic clocks, and quantum sensors to telecommunications infrastructure.
- The results establish key components needed for future applications like quantum cryptography, distributed quantum computing, and quantum data centers.
